summary: Iteration 28: Complete validation of White-Label Phase 1 implementation. Backend 100% passed (9/9 tests), Frontend 100% passed. All white-label features working correctly including GET/PUT/DELETE APIs, Settings > White Label tab, palette presets, color pickers, button radius options, preview panel, save/reset functionality, and sidebar color application.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/brands/{brand_id}/white-label returns default (empty) config with enabled=false
Backend: GET /api/brands/{brand_id}/white-label requires authentication (401)
Backend: PUT /api/brands/{brand_id}/white-label saves colors and returns enabled=true
Backend: PUT /api/brands/{brand_id}/white-label changes persist in database (verified via GET)
Backend: PUT /api/brands/{brand_id}/white-label requires authentication (401)
Backend: PUT /api/brands/{brand_id}/white-label with empty payload returns 400
Backend: DELETE /api/brands/{brand_id}/white-label resets to defaults
Backend: DELETE /api/brands/{brand_id}/white-label requires authentication (401)
Backend: GET /api/brands/invalid_brand_id/white-label returns 404
Frontend: Settings page loads with 'White Label' tab visible
Frontend: Clicking 'White Label' tab shows WhiteLabelSettings component
Frontend: Status badge shows 'Inativo' by default
Frontend: 6 quick palette buttons rendered (Azul Corporativo, Verde Natureza, Roxo Premium, Vermelho Energia, Laranja Criativo, Rose Elegante)
Frontend: 4 color pickers render (primary, accent, sidebar, sidebar text)
Frontend: 5 button radius options render (Reto, Suave, Medio, Arredondado, Pill)
Frontend: Preview button toggles preview panel
Frontend: Clicking a palette pre-fills the color fields
Frontend: Save White-Label button sends PUT request and shows success toast
Frontend: After save, status badge changes to 'Ativo'
Frontend: After save, Reset button appears
Frontend: After save, sidebar reflects the custom sidebar_color (verified rgb(15, 23, 42) = #0F172A)
Frontend: Reset button sends DELETE request and reverts to default
Frontend: After reset, status badge changes back to 'Inativo'
Frontend: Logo upload section is present
Frontend: Navigation to Dashboard works after white-label is applied
Frontend: Navigation to Planning works after white-label is applied
Frontend: Navigation to Campaigns works after white-label is applied
critical_code_review_comments: white_label.py: Correctly implements GET/PUT/DELETE endpoints with proper authentication and permission checks
white_label.py: PUT endpoint sets enabled=true automatically when colors are saved
white_label.py: DELETE endpoint uses $unset to remove white_label field from brand document
WhiteLabelSettings.js: Well-structured component with 6 preset palettes and 4 color pickers
WhiteLabelSettings.js: Uses data-testid attributes for all interactive elements (good testability)
WhiteLabelSettings.js: Preview panel shows live preview of sidebar and button styles
WhiteLabelContext.js: Correctly applies CSS variables (--wl-primary, --wl-accent, --wl-sidebar-bg, --wl-sidebar-text, --wl-radius) when config.enabled is true
WhiteLabelContext.js: Properly removes CSS variables when white-label is disabled
Layout.js: Sidebar correctly uses wlConfig.sidebar_color and wlConfig.sidebar_text_color for custom styling
Layout.js: Shows brand logo when white-label is enabled and logo_url exists
App.js: WhiteLabelProvider correctly wrapped inside AppPage (ProtectedRoute > BrandProvider > PermissionProvider > WhiteLabelProvider > MainLayout)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 28 validated White-Label Phase 1 implementation. Key findings: 1) Backend endpoints at /api/brands/{brand_id}/white-label (GET/PUT/DELETE) all working correctly with proper auth, 2) Frontend WhiteLabelSettings component in Settings > White Label tab renders all UI elements (6 palettes, 4 color pickers, 5 radius options, preview panel, save/reset buttons), 3) WhiteLabelContext applies CSS variables dynamically when enabled, 4) Layout.js sidebar uses custom colors when white-label is active, 5) Navigation to other pages works correctly after white-label is applied. NO MOCKED APIs - all endpoints are real. Tutorial modal appears on first login - need to click through 'Próximo' buttons and 'Começar!' to dismiss.
notes: White-Label Phase 1 is fully functional. The feature allows brands to customize: primary color, accent color, sidebar background color, sidebar text color, and button border radius. The 6 preset palettes provide quick customization options. The preview panel shows a live preview before saving. After saving, the sidebar immediately reflects the custom colors. The reset functionality properly reverts all settings to defaults.
